meta.reviewer_notes: philatelist is held out here and reappears taught in wd-0007 (-ist): the source itself derives it from both roots, so the cross-link is faithful, not invented. Owner review pending.

unit.label: phil
unit.mentor_note: Phil never announces itself loudly. It just quietly means someone loves the thing standing beside it.

members.meaning: an admirer of British culture and people
one who collects or studies stamps

discovery.understand_note: Phil means to love, and it always attaches to the thing being loved. Philosophy is love of wisdom, philanthropy is love of humanity, a bibliophile loves books, an Anglophile loves British culture. The root never changes; only the object of its affection does.

applies.prompt: Knowing phil- means to love, what do you think a philatelist loves?
